description.name: Canopy Nest Treehouse
description.headline: Solar-powered jungle treehouse with private waterfall access in the Osa Peninsula
description.description: Perched 9 metres above the rainforest floor in the Osa Peninsula, Canopy Nest is a hand-crafted treehouse built entirely from reclaimed hardwood. The open-air sleeping platform lets you fall asleep to the sound of the forest and wake to howler monkeys and toucans. Solar power, rainwater collection, and a composting system make this a genuinely low-impact stay. A private trail leads to a natural swimming pool at the base of a 12-metre waterfall.

reviews: The most unique and magical place I have ever slept. Falling asleep to the jungle was life-changing.
The waterfall is a 10-minute walk and completely private. Saw three species of monkey from the deck.
Completely off-grid but surprisingly comfortable. The hosts thought of everything. Unforgettable.

policies.cancellation_policy: Free cancellation up to 14 days before check-in
